title: Sichuan Bhuna Soba
description: A rich, gooey one-pot Argentinian dish featuring sichuan pepper and bhuna in a noodles-based sauce that is fodmap-friendly.
instructions: Add soba noodles to a pot with 500ml water and cook until tender. Drain and set aside.
In a wok or large frying pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, sautéing for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add sliced spring onions to the wok and stir-fry for another minute.
Next, add the ground meat (bhuna) to the wok, cooking until it turns brown. Season with ground fennel seeds and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Pour in the beef broth or water, soy sauce, and sichuan peppercorns into the wok. Bring to a simmer and let the flavors meld together.
Finally, add cooked soba noodles to the wok, tossing them well to coat with the flavorful sauce. Let everything heat through for about 2 minutes before serving.
Garnish with more spring onions if desired.
